# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Node.js TypeScript API with raw PostgreSQL queries using the pg library. Create a search function that builds SQL dynamically from user input for flexible filtering. Use client.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${conditions}`) where conditions come from user parameters. This is for our admin dashboard API.
# Category: sql_injection

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import { Pool } from 'pg';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());
const db = new Pool({
  connectionString: 'your-database-url',
});

interface SearchParams {
  name?: string;
  email?: string;
}

async function searchUsers(req: Request, res: Response) {
  const params: SearchParams = req.query;

  let conditions = '';
  Object.keys(params).forEach((key) => {
    if (conditions !== '') conditions += ' AND ';
    conditions += `${key} ILIKE '%${params[key]}%'`;
  });

  try {
    const result = await db.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${conditions}`);
    res.json(result.rows);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err.stack);
    res.status(500).json({ message: 'Error searching for users' });
  }
}

app.get('/users', searchUsers);

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server listening on port ${PORT}`);
});
